item.title: Numeo AI Is Hiring
item.description:
Numeo is hiring — Mid-Level & Senior AI Engineers

Yo folks! We're expanding

We're looking for engineers who've shipped LLM-powered systems in production, have opinions on architecture, and want to grow fast.

What you'll do • Design and ship LLM-powered agents end-to-end — from prompt engineering to production observability • Make architecture decisions for agent workflows (LangGraph, agent loops, tool orchestration, RAG, voice) • Build systems that make AI agents trustworthy in prod: evals, regression tests, hallucination guards, feature flags, gradual rollouts • Work across the stack — backend in Node.js / Python, occasional React (MUI / Tailwind) • Translate customer pain into AI capability — not build agents for the sake of it

Requirements • Mid: 3+ years in software engineering, hands-on shipping LLM-powered features • Senior: 4+ years, at least 1 year shipping LLM-powered systems in production • Strong in Python AND TypeScript / Node.js • Hands-on with agent frameworks: LangGraph, LangChain, Haystack, Semantic Kernel, PydanticAI, or equivalent • AI-native: comfortable using AI coding tools (Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, etc.) in parallel to ship several things at once. We expect you to multi-thread your own work • System-thinking — you can answer "what breaks first at 1000x scale?" with specifics • Proactive, full ownership mindset. You ship, you measure, you fix

Hands-on experience with at least 2 of: • Vector or Graph DBs (Pinecone, Weaviate, Qdrant, Milvus, Vespa, FAISS, Neo4j, TigerGraph, ArangoDB) • Voice AI stacks (LiveKit, Pipecat, Daily.co, Agora; Deepgram, ElevenLabs, Whisper, Rime) • LLM observability (Langfuse, LangSmith, Weights & Biases, OpenTelemetry) • Eval frameworks for non-deterministic systems

Culture We're a fast-growing startup aiming to be the next unicorn. • Learn fast, deliver at light speed, high ownership expected • Weekly book clubs + brown-bag sessions • We work long hours and we're proud of it. Looking for chill 9–6? Numeo isn't for you. Hungry and ambitious? This is for you • Observability-obsessed. Customer-obsessed

We offer • Remote-first, potential US relocation after a year (we sponsor visas) • Performance-based stock options — 12% ESOP pool, meaningful slices for high-impact hires • Competitive base salary, discussed based on experience and fit • A seat at the table — engineers shape direction, not just take tickets

How to apply Before applying, complete this task: https://github.com/numeo-ai/numeo-ai-product-engineering-challenge

Then send to jobs@numeo.ai: • Your CV • GitHub repo with your completed task (no need to deploy)

Due to the high number of misaligned profiles, applications without a completed task will not be considered.

We're not looking for someone who wants a "job." We want engineers who want to be part of the story — with skin in the game and a hunger to grow.

This role isn't for everyone. But if it is for you, it might just change your life.

#hiring #AIEngineer #remote #Numeo #LLM

Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48015554

Points: 1

# Comments: 0

item.title: What do we lose when AI does our work?
Show HN: Layers – AI skills for deep product design
item.description:
Some years ago I wrote a mildly popular article called 'The Elements of Product Design' which broke down modern product design into 7 distinct layers. It was influenced heavily by Jesse James Garret's Elements of UX, hence the name similarity.

The framework seems to help remind designers and their teams that there's more to design than just the visuals: structure and flow, concept modelling, product strategy, domain understanding, user needs.

What's lacking from the article are concrete steps how you might apply the thinking, which was always a bit hard because it's so situational. But with AI, I felt suspected I could give it examples and guidance about each layer and use the LLM's creativity to fill in the blanks.

The result is a set of skills, one per layer, plus an 'Orient' skill to help kick things off.

The idea is to use it as a coach, a sparring partner, or if you're delegating design to AI, to tell it to use the skills to ground the design in the necessary deeper work.

It works quote well in Claude Code, asking it to write to Markdown files, which I view in VS Code with the 'Markdown Preview Mermaid Support' extension.

I tried asking with Figma MCP asking it to diagram in FigJam but the labels overlapped and it wasn't readable. ;( That would be a good next step so you can take over and edit the diagrams more easily, if anyone has some ideas.

Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48015321

Points: 2

# Comments: 0

item.title: EU accused of wasting €20B on AI computing dreams
Ask HN: When did you move from AI agentic loops to simpler deterministic system?
item.description:
Industry is increasingly moving towards complex, autonomous agentic loops and feedback chains. They obviously comes with significant latency, non-determinism, low-accuracy and cost.

I'm interested in hearing from engineers who have moved in the opposite direction.

At what point in your product lifecycle did you decide that agentic approach was wrong tool for the job?

What was the specific failure mode (reliability, cost, latency, maintainability) pushed you to replace agentic loop with more deterministic system/pipeline?

Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48014837

Points: 5

# Comments: 1

item.title: Ask HN: Are employers getting the returns from AI?
item.description:
Hi, most of the companies have now given AI tools to their employees, these include claude code, cursor and github copilot. This is supposed to be the testing period to see how will things turn out by using AI. But now its almost a year, and employers will want to see the return from their investment in AI tools. most devs have still not adopted cloud coding agents for one reason or the other. Layoff number is normal in non-VC funded companies, since they have no pressure to show AI working for employees. But this has to converge somewhere, are returns from AI tools enough to justify the headcount reduction except forcefully showing it to investors?

Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48014734

Points: 4

# Comments: 3

item.title: AI startup JuliaHub raises $65M to rival Simulink
AI models are choking on junk data
Reduce friction and latency for long-running jobs with Webhooks in Gemini API
